ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers cream puff food products broadly; no specific code exists for caramel pudding-filled mini choux puffs. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers choux pastry as a category. No specific code exists for caramel pudding mini puffs. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific confectionery pastry product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one box of 12 mini puffs, estimated at approximately 180 g total (approximately 15 g per puff), as sold by Kazo Premium at SGD 16.90.

nutrient_source.title: Cream puff (choux pastry with custard/pudding filling) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods; supplemented by analogous choux pastry with cream filling data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match for caramel pudding mini puff exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cream puffs with custard filling (FDC ID 18960) and choux pastry analogues, adjusted for caramel pudding filling composition. Caramel pudding filling increases sugar content relative to plain custard. Values represent a reasonable central estimate for a baked choux shell filled with caramel pudding cream.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of choux pastry and cream-filled pastry products — analogous estimate based on custard-filled pastry and refined wheat flour pastry GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for caramel pudding mini puffs. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for custard-filled pastry and refined wheat flour choux pastry analogues. Caramel pudding filling contains sucrose and cooked starch, consistent with medium GI range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 65.7) / 100 ≈ 42.7, where carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 36.5 × 1.8 = 65.7 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 180 g serving provides approximately 333 mg sodium and 14.8 g saturated fat; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and frequency of consumption of this high-calorie pastry.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 65 and approximately 65.7 g of carbohydrate per box serving (including 32.4 g sugar), this product poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A single box serving contributes approximately 14.8 g saturated fat, 0.36 g trans fat, and 171 mg cholesterol, all of which are clinically significant for cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and monitor overall saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Caramel Pudding Mini Puff is a premium dessert product sold in Singapore, consisting of 12 bite-sized choux pastry shells filled with a smooth caramel pudding cream. The choux shells are baked until golden and crisp on the outside, while the interior is soft and hollow, allowing the rich caramel-flavoured pudding filling to provide a contrasting creamy texture. The caramel filling is typically made from cooked sugar, milk, eggs, and butter, giving it a deep amber sweetness with a silky mouthfeel. Each mini puff is approximately 15 g, making the full box approximately 180 g. The product is sold by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall in Singapore at SGD 16.90 per box. Nutritionally, the product is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (36.5 g/100 g), sugars (18 g/100 g), and fat (16.5 g/100 g including 8.2 g saturated fat). The medium glycemic index (estimated 65) and high sugar load make this product a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with these allergens. The product is considered halal and vegetarian. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.

Is the caramel pudding mini puff halal?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional match for this specific commercial product exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cream puff with custard filling data (FDC ID 18960) and adjusted for caramel pudding filling. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for similar pastry products. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cream puff with custard filling (FDC ID 18960) as the closest approved analogue; adjusted upward for sugar content to reflect caramel pudding filling. Serving size of 180 g (12 × ~15 g puffs) is estimated from typical mini choux puff dimensions and the product count. Vitamin B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cream puff entry used as the analogue source. Vitamin C is 0.0 as choux pastry with caramel pudding filling contains no vitamin C by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central analogue data. Trans fat is present at a low level (0.2 g/100 g) consistent with butter-containing pastry. Halal status assumed based on typical Kazo Premium product formulation; not independently verified. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no peer-reviewed direct measurement of this specific product was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 65.7) / 100 ≈ 42.8 g, where carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 65.7 g.
